title: Vietnamese Iced Coffee

ingredients.name: ground dark roast coffee (Vietnamese robusta or strong espresso blend)
visual_checks: coarsely ground; Vietnamese Trung Nguyen or Bru is ideal; instant coffee works as backup
ingredients.notes: coarsely ground; Vietnamese Trung Nguyen or Bru is ideal; instant coffee works as backup

subs.name: 2 tsp instant coffee dissolved in 100 ml hot water for a quick version
subs.notes: flavour will be lighter but the method is the same

ingredients.name: sweetened condensed milk
visual_checks: 2 tbsp per glass; adjust to taste
ingredients.notes: 2 tbsp per glass; adjust to taste

subs.name: 3 tsp sugar plus 2 tbsp cream or full-fat milk for a less sweet version

ingredients.name: hot water (just off the boil)
visual_checks: 100 ml per glass; use 93-96°C water for best extraction
ingredients.notes: 100 ml per glass; use 93-96°C water for best extraction

ingredients.name: ice cubes (generous amount)
visual_checks: about a full glass of ice per serving; keeps the drink cold without diluting immediately
ingredients.notes: about a full glass of ice per serving; keeps the drink cold without diluting immediately

ingredients.name: milk (optional, for a lighter version)
visual_checks: for ca phe sua nong (hot version), skip the ice and stir milk in after the condensed milk
ingredients.notes: for ca phe sua nong (hot version), skip the ice and stir milk in after the condensed milk

subs.name: coconut condensed milk for a dairy-free version
subs.notes: available online and at specialty stores

steps.text: If you have a Vietnamese phin filter: place 2 tablespoons of ground coffee in the filter over a glass. Add 100 ml of hot water (about 95°C) and cover. Let it drip slowly for 4-5 minutes — the brew will be dark, intense and syrupy. If you do not have a phin filter, brew double-strength coffee by making a very concentrated French press or Moka pot coffee: use double the normal amount of coffee grounds.
While the coffee brews, place 2 tablespoons of condensed milk at the bottom of each glass. The condensed milk goes in first so it melts into the hot coffee rather than sitting on top of cold milk.
Pour the hot brewed coffee directly over the condensed milk. Stir briefly so the milk dissolves into the coffee. Taste — it should be intensely strong and very sweet; the ice will dilute it slightly.

Fill a tall glass generously with ice cubes. Pour the hot coffee-condensed milk mixture over the ice all at once — the contrast of hot coffee hitting cold ice creates a slightly frothy, chilled drink instantly. Stir once with a long spoon and serve immediately.

Drink slowly. The condensed milk settles and the ice melts gradually, subtly changing the drink's sweetness and strength as you go. This is part of the ritual. For the hot version (ca phe sua nong), skip the ice and serve in a small cup.

cross_contact_notes: Ingredient metadata cannot prove an allergen-free kitchen. Check packaged labels and cross-contact statements for your products.
trust.provenance_summary: AI-assisted original draft; declared licence original.
trust.substitution_warning: A substitution can introduce allergens or change dietary suitability. Never claim a swap is safe without checking the replacement's label and the user's allergy context.

substitution_notes: condensed-milk → 3 tsp sugar plus 2 tbsp cream or full-fat milk for a less sweet version; coffee → 2 tsp instant coffee dissolved in 100 ml hot water for a quick version (flavour will be lighter but the method is the same); milk → coconut condensed milk for a dairy-free version (available online and at specialty stores)
indian_kitchen_adaptation: South Indian filter coffee lovers will feel right at home — the double-decoction from a Moka pot or Bru/Nescafe strong brew makes an excellent substitute for Vietnamese drip coffee. Milkmaid (sweetened condensed milk) is at every Indian kirana store and supermarket. The only equipment needed is a kettle and two glasses. For the most authentic result, try Vietnamese robusta coffee brands (Trung Nguyen, G7) available on Amazon India.
